bullet_summary: The cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak arrived off Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands, where the passengers and some of the crew will begin disembarking on Sunday, officials said
Nobody among more than 140 people on board the MV Hondius is showing symptoms of the virus, Spanish authorities, the World Health Organization and cruise company Oceanwide Expeditions said
Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship

items.content: Spanish passengers are the first to leave the MV Hondius, after an outbreak saw three people die and several infected.

items.title: International evacuation underway as cruise ship passengers return home
items.standfirst: Spanish passengers and crew from the quarantined ship have begun disembarking as an international evacuation effort unfolds in the Canary Islands. Authorities say strict health protocols are in place, while officials and experts stress the virus poses far less risk than COVID-19 despite growing public concern. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports.
items.content: Spanish passengers and crew from the quarantined ship have begun disembarking as an international evacuation effort unfolds in the Canary Islands. Authorities say strict health protocols are in place, while officials and experts stress the virus poses far less risk than COVID-19 despite growing public concern. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports.

items.title: Live: First plane with passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship departs for Madrid
items.standfirst: The first plane carrying passengers evacuated from the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius cruise ship has departed Tenerife for a hospital in Madrid. Spanish passengers began arriving on shore on Sunday morning, with nationals from other ​countries to follow. Follow our live blog for all the latest updates.
items.content: The first plane carrying passengers evacuated from the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius cruise ship has departed Tenerife for a hospital in Madrid. Spanish passengers began arriving on shore on Sunday morning, with nationals from other ​countries to follow. Follow our live blog for all the latest updates.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives at Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak arrived off Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands, where the passengers and some of the crew will begin disembarking on Sunday, officials said. Nobody among more than 140 people on board the MV Hondius is showing symptoms of the virus, Spanish authorities, the World Health Organization and cruise company Oceanwide Expeditions said. Three people have died since the outbreak, and five passengers who left the ship are infected with hantavirus, which can cause life-threatening illness. The ship will not dock but remain anchored.
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak arrived off Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands, where the passengers and some of the crew will begin disembarking on Sunday, officials said. Nobody among more than 140 people on board the MV Hondius is showing symptoms of the virus, Spanish authorities, the World Health Organization and cruise company Oceanwide Expeditions said. Three people have died since the outbreak, and five passengers who left the ship are infected with hantavirus, which can cause life-threatening illness. The ship will not dock but remain anchored.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship disembarking in Canary Islands
items.standfirst: Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents, but in rare cases it can be transmitted from person to person.
items.content: Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents, but in rare cases it can be transmitted from person to person.

items.title: Passengers begin disembarking from cruise ship hit by hantavirus
items.standfirst: Passengers began disembarking on Sunday from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak which was anchored near Tenerife. Three passengers from the MV Hondius have died but health officials stress that the risk to global public health is low.
items.content: Passengers began disembarking on Sunday from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak which was anchored near Tenerife. Three passengers from the MV Hondius have died but health officials stress that the risk to global public health is low.

items.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives in Tenerife
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.

items.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
items.standfirst: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.
items.content: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.

items.title: Hantavirus cruise passengers to be evacuated to home countries, including Americans
items.standfirst: In just hours, the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is expected to arrive in the Canary Islands. All the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries, including 17 Americans. Ramy Inocencio reports and Dr. Jon LaPook has more on the virus.
items.content: In just hours, the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is expected to arrive in the Canary Islands. All the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries, including 17 Americans. Ramy Inocencio reports and Dr. Jon LaPook has more on the virus.

items.title: European nations dispatch planes to evacuate citizens from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.standfirst: Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, and the Netherlands will send planes to evacuate their citizens aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship ​hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak, Spain's interior minister said on Saturday, adding that transport will also be arranged by the European Union, the US and the UK for the remaining passengers.
items.content: Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, and the Netherlands will send planes to evacuate their citizens aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship ​hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak, Spain's interior minister said on Saturday, adding that transport will also be arranged by the European Union, the US and the UK for the remaining passengers.

items.title: Spanish island braces for hantavirus cruise ship as WHO urges calm amid protests
items.standfirst: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage
items.content: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage

items.title: Contact tracing begins after Hantavirus-hit cruise passengers disembarks in St. Helena
items.standfirst: Health authorities have launched international contact tracing efforts following three deaths aboard a cruise ship linked to suspected hantavirus exposure. Officials are monitoring passengers who disembarked in St. Helena, while suspected cases in Alicante and Barcelona remain under observation pending test results. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports from Madrid.
items.content: Health authorities have launched international contact tracing efforts following three deaths aboard a cruise ship linked to suspected hantavirus exposure. Officials are monitoring passengers who disembarked in St. Helena, while suspected cases in Alicante and Barcelona remain under observation pending test results. FRANCE 24's Sarah Morris reports from Madrid.

items.title: 7 states prepare to receive Americans possibly exposed to hantavirus
items.standfirst: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.
items.content: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.

items.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
items.standfirst: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.
items.content: Health officials have identified at least nine confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

clusters.title: Russian President Putin accuses West of arming Ukraine on Victory Day
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian President Putin accuses West of arming Ukraine on Victory Day
bullet_summary: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end
Reported by France24, CBS News, Sky News World and others

items.title: Russian President Putin accuses West of arming Ukraine on Victory Day
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that the conflict in Ukraine is nearing its end after he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Talking to reporters, Putin accused Western “globalist elites” of arming Ukraine to defeat and break up Russia and declared that “I think the matter is coming to an end.”
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that the conflict in Ukraine is nearing its end after he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Talking to reporters, Putin accused Western “globalist elites” of arming Ukraine to defeat and break up Russia and declared that “I think the matter is coming to an end.”

items.title: Putin says Ukrainian conflict nearing end amid Victory Day celebrations
items.standfirst: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.
items.content: The Victory Day celebrations didn't include a show of heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war likely to end soon - despite Ukrainian battlefield gains
items.standfirst: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.
items.content: President Vladimir Putin has said he believes the Ukraine war is coming to an end.

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war 'heading to an end' despite ceasefire violations
items.standfirst: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."
items.content: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. Russian President Vladimir Putin, however, asserted at a press conference later that evening that the war was "heading to an end."

items.title: Moscow marks Victory Day amid tight security
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II – a show that didn't include heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades. Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, which was scaled down even as a US-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festivities.

items.title: North Korean troops join Putin’s scaled-back parade as Ukraine agrees temporary ceasefire
items.standfirst: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany
items.content: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany

clusters.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era

bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, Sky News World

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
items.standfirst: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Iran warns of retaliation as US tensions threaten fragile peace talks
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran warns of retaliation as US tensions threaten fragile peace talks

items.title: Iran warns of retaliation as US tensions threaten fragile peace talks
items.standfirst: Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated after reports of US fire on Iranian-linked tankers in the Gulf of Oman. Tehran warned of retaliation against American targets in the region as diplomatic efforts continue over a proposed deal involving sanctions, nuclear limits, and maritime security.
items.content: Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated after reports of US fire on Iranian-linked tankers in the Gulf of Oman. Tehran warned of retaliation against American targets in the region as diplomatic efforts continue over a proposed deal involving sanctions, nuclear limits, and maritime security.

items.title: Live: Iran's Guards threaten US sites in the region if its tankers come under fire
items.standfirst: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington continued to wait for Tehran’s response to its latest proposal for a peace deal. Follow our live blog for all the latest updates.
items.content: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington continued to wait for Tehran’s response to its latest proposal for a peace deal. items.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten US sites in Middle East if tankers come under fire
items.standfirst: Revolutionary Guards issue warning as Trump awaits Iran’s response to Washington’s latest proposal for peace dealIran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington was left waiting for Tehran’s response to its latest negotiating position.“Any attack on Iranian tankers and commercial vessels will result in a heavy attack on one of the American centres in the region and enemy ships,” the force said, a day after US strikes on two Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Starmer vows to stay on after local elections deliver losses for Labour
clusters.neutral_headline: Starmer vows to stay on after local elections deliver losses for Labour

items.title: Starmer vows to stay on after local elections deliver losses for Labour
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer vowed Friday to remain as Britain's prime minister after disastrous local elections saw his centre-left Labour party humiliated across the UK, with disillusioned voters backing hard-right and nationalist parties. Thursday's ballots – Starmer's biggest electoral test since Labour ousted the Conservatives in 2024 – left the British leader under intense pressure after the party suffered a historic mauling in its Welsh heartlands.
items.content: Keir Starmer vowed Friday to remain as Britain's prime minister after disastrous local elections saw his centre-left Labour party humiliated across the UK, with disillusioned voters backing hard-right and nationalist parties. Thursday's ballots – Starmer's biggest electoral test since Labour ousted the Conservatives in 2024 – left the British leader under intense pressure after the party suffered a historic mauling in its Welsh heartlands.

items.title: U.K.'s Starmer faces calls to resign after disastrous local elections
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged Saturday to revive the Labour Party after a disastrous set of local and regional elections.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged Saturday to revive the Labour Party after a disastrous set of local and regional elections.

clusters.title: At least 15 killed in car bomb attack on northwest Pakistan checkpoint
clusters.neutral_headline: Deadly car bomb attack hits checkpoint in northwest Pakistan
bullet_summary: Militants detonated a car bomb at a checkpoint in northwestern Pakistan and opened fire on police, killing at least 15 and wounding three, officials said on Sunday
Gunmen stormed the police checkpost after the car blast, opening fire, according to officials who said they also used small drones in the attack

items.title: At least 15 killed in car bomb attack on northwest Pakistan checkpoint
items.standfirst: Militants detonated a car bomb at a checkpoint in northwestern Pakistan and opened fire on police, killing at least 15 and wounding three, officials said on Sunday. Gunmen stormed the police checkpost after the car blast, opening fire, according to officials who said they also used small drones in the attack.
items.content: Militants detonated a car bomb at a checkpoint in northwestern Pakistan and opened fire on police, killing at least 15 and wounding three, officials said on Sunday. Gunmen stormed the police checkpost after the car blast, opening fire, according to officials who said they also used small drones in the attack.

items.standfirst: A suspected car bombing followed by a firefight at a police checkpoint has killed more than a dozen people in Bannu.
